MARETO HQ  —  CORE MODULES OVERVIEW USER GUIDE
Navigating Mareto
Core Modules Overview — User Guide

About This Guide

This guide provides a step-by-step overview of how to navigate Mareto, including your user menu, profile settings, reminders, and each of the core modules available in the left-hand navigation. It is intended for frontline users and new staff who are beginning to use the system.

Before you begin

You will need your Mareto login credentials. These are provided by your system administrator. Once you have them, you are ready to access your workspace.

Module Quick Reference

Module Purpose
HomeWelcome page with live analytics, quick links, and announcements.
Person RegistryCentralized record of all individuals connected to your organization.
IntakeCaptures point-in-time information when a participant enters your program.
CaseHolds all information about a participant's activities within a specific program.
Event LogPoint-in-time records of activities, notes, and interactions associated with a case.
AppointmentTracks upcoming meetings and scheduled interactions.
ReferralsManages referrals made within or outside your organization.
CalendarDate-based view of appointments, reminders, intake dates, and case milestones.

1. Accessing Your Workspace

To access your Mareto board, navigate to your organization's Mareto login page. Click on your organization's logo in the center of the page. This will take you directly into your workspace.

Login screen — click your organization's board to enter your workspace

2. Your User Menu

In the upper right-hand corner of any page, you will find your initials. Clicking your initials opens your user menu, which gives you access to several account options.

From the user menu you can access:

  • Reminders — set personal or team reminders with date and time notifications
  • Profile — update your name, avatar, and display preferences
  • Change Password — update your account password
  • Theme — switch your display theme
  • Logout — sign out of your session

3. Setting Reminders

Reminders allow you to set personal notifications for yourself or a colleague. To create a reminder, click your initials in the top right corner, then select Reminders, then Add a Reminder.

Completing the Reminder Form

Fill in the following fields:

  • Title — a short label for the reminder
  • Notes — any additional context you want to capture
  • Date — click the calendar icon to select a date
  • Time — set a specific time for the notification
  • Assignee — defaults to your own account; can be assigned to a colleague if your role allows

Navigating the Date Picker

Within the date picker, you can navigate dates using the following controls:

  • Arrow closest to center — toggles between months
  • Center arrow — toggles between years
  • Far right arrow — toggles through decades

Saving and Receiving Reminders

Click Save to create the reminder. When the scheduled time arrives, the reminder will appear in your right-hand panel.

Note on reminder behaviour

Reminders cannot be permanently saved once triggered. When notified, you can either delay the reminder or dismiss it.

4. Managing Your Profile

To access your profile settings, click your initials in the top right corner and select Profile.

From your profile you can:

  • Update your avatar and choose custom avatar text and background colours
  • Update your display name
  • Change your password via the Security tab
  • Log out via the Logout option

Once you have made your changes, scroll down and click Update Profile to save them. To return to your main workspace, click Menu in the top right corner.

5. Main Navigation

The left-hand sidebar is your primary way to move between modules in Mareto. It is visible from any page within your workspace.

Collapsing and Expanding the Menu

In the corner of the navigation menu, you will notice an X. Clicking this collapses the menu into a full-size screen, giving you more working space. This is especially useful when working on a tablet or smaller device in the field.

To expand the menu again, click the hamburger icon (three horizontal lines) in the top left corner.

Configuration note

Your available navigation items may vary depending on your organization's setup and your role within the system. The modules described in this guide reflect the standard configuration.

6. Home

The Home screen is your welcome page. It provides a high-level view of activity within your program area, including live counts of People, Cases, and Events. Hovering over any of the summary tiles will display a tooltip confirming what each number represents.

Announcements Panel

On the right-hand side of the Home screen, you may see an announcements or communications panel. Your organization uses this space to share updates, team notices, or internal reminders. Announcements are colour-coded by priority. To create a new announcement, click New Announcement and complete the required fields.

7. Person Registry

The Person Registry contains records of all individuals connected to your organization. This may include clients, visitors, volunteers, and staff. It is shared across your entire organization and acts as a single source of truth for person-level data.

Important — always search before creating

Before creating a new person record, always search for the individual first. This prevents duplicate entries and fragmented data across your organization.

To add a new person, click the New Person Registration button and fill out all required fields.

+ New Person Registration

8. Intake

An intake typically occurs after a person record is created but before a case is opened. It may represent a decision point in your organization, or capture point-in-time information when a participant enters your program. A person can have more than one intake during their time with your organization.

Creating a New Intake

To create a new intake, click Add. Complete all required fields before saving.

A standard intake typically includes:

  • Primary Person — the individual the intake is for
  • Intake Status and Phase
  • Start date and end date
  • Intake Supervisor
  • Referral Source
  • Notes

Once all required fields are complete, click Save.

9. Case

Think of a case as a folder that holds all information about a participant and their activities within your program. From the Case module you can search for existing cases, view case statuses, and create new cases.

Cases are program-specific

Cases are not shared across programs. If a client has a case in one program, another program within your organization may not be able to see that case or its associated information.

Case File Fields

A case file typically includes:

  • Primary Person — the participant linked to the case
  • Program — the specific program the case belongs to
  • Case Status
  • Case Supervisor
  • Start date and end date
  • Risk Assessments, Legal and Care, and Case Plan details
  • Room assignments, where applicable

10. Event Log

Events are point-in-time records associated with a client or a case. They capture what happened, when, and in what context. Examples of events include:

  • Referrals
  • Signed agreements
  • Completed consents
  • Case notes
  • Resources provided
  • Other program activities
Event Log guidelines

Events are generally not edited after they are logged. They are unique to the program and case they are associated with — events logged in one program are not visible to another program unless specifically configured to be shared.

Creating an Event

To log a new event, click Add from the Event Log. Complete the required fields including:

  • Event start and end date and time
  • Primary person
  • Event type
  • Associated case or intake

The Event Type field determines what sub-fields appear.

11. Appointments

The Appointments module allows you to track upcoming meetings and scheduled interactions with clients or colleagues. You can view appointments in a list format or switch to a calendar view. To create a new appointment, click Add and complete the required fields.

12. Referrals

The Referrals section displays all referrals made within or outside your organization. You can filter referrals by status to manage and track referral progress.

Available referral statuses include:

  • Pending
  • In Review
  • Accepted
  • Waitlisted
  • Declined
  • Referred Out
  • Withdrawn by Referrer or by Client
  • Duplicate
  • Other

13. Calendar

The Calendar reflects any date-based records collected within Mareto, depending on your organization's configuration. This may include:

  • Appointments
  • Reminders
  • Intake dates
  • Case milestones such as consent expiry or agreement review dates

You can navigate by date using the month, week, day, or agenda views and use the calendar to manage your schedule and workload.

Getting More Familiar with Mareto

As you continue using Mareto, you will become more familiar with how your specific organization has configured the system to support your work. Additional how-to videos and user guides are available in the Mareto resource library. If you need help, contact your system administrator.
